notes: 64-bit; 2x power of prior SPARC; announced fall 1995; UltraSPARC servers shipping May 1996; superseded by x86 in Oracle/Sun portfolio
Fine-tuned SMP: 3-8% CPU overhead vs 15-30% for NT; threaded I/O and networking; SMP scalability 92-97% efficiency; succeeded by Solaris 2.6, 7, 8...
2.6 GBps; thin boards with dual CPUs and memory (up to 2GB/board); high-speed I/O channels; mainframe-class speeds
Industry-standard fiber channels; hot-swap disk modules, fans, power supplies; 4.2GB drives (3.5 inch backplane); Aberdeen cites superior MTBF vs competitors
Oracle 7.3 supported distributed parallel query on SMCC clusters; 5.5TB Oracle demonstration at SMCC test center
Aberdeen noted NT Server scalability 'pales' vs Solaris for data warehousing; NT SMP overhead up to 30% vs Solaris 3-8%
